families.name: Oncofetal vs hepatic synthetic balance
families.rationale: HCC re-expresses oncofetal AFP/GPC3 while losing mature hepatocyte synthetic program (ALB/APOA1), giving a high-dynamic-range tumor-vs-normal contrast.
families.skeptic_test: Reject if delta_baseline < 0.10 vs ALB-only logistic baseline or permutation p > 0.01.

families.name: Proliferation-to-hepatic-function contrast
families.rationale: Normal liver is post-mitotic with high ALB; tumors gain MKI67/TOP2A — a near-orthogonal axis to oncofetal markers.
families.skeptic_test: Reject if AUROC drops below 0.90 on held-out folds or delta over single-gene MKI67 < 0.03.

families.name: Warburg glycolytic shift ratio
families.rationale: Tumor metabolic reprogramming raises glycolytic enzymes/transporters while hepatic acute-phase AHSG falls, encoding a metabolism-vs-secretion contrast.
families.skeptic_test: Reject if permutation p > 0.05 or if law is dominated (>90% variance explained) by AHSG alone, indicating no glycolytic signal.

families.name: HIF/hypoxia axis contrast
families.rationale: HIF-driven CA9/NDUFA4L2 mark hypoxic tumor niches absent from well-perfused normal liver expressing TTR.
families.skeptic_test: Reject if delta_baseline < 0.05 vs TTR-only or if CA9/NDUFA4L2 show bimodal-but-non-discriminative behavior (cohen_d < 0.5).

families.name: Housekeeping-gene contrast (negative control)
families.rationale: Housekeeping genes are by design invariant across conditions; this contrast should carry minimal class information.
families.skeptic_test: Reject if AUROC > 0.65 or permutation p < 0.05, which would indicate housekeeping normalization assumption is violated in this panel.
